answer:
\boxed{D}  

Compound meters have beat subdivisions into three equal parts, and 12/16 fits this as four dotted sixteenth-note beats, each divided into three thirty-second notes. In contrast, 11/8 and 7/4 are irregular or odd meters, while 8/8 is typically simple or irregular. Thus, 12/16 is the only compound meter among the options.
